title: MMR ETHUSDT 4h: Natural 4h bar size provides adequate hold time for mean-reversion
content: Prior MMR ETH 1h null-screen (hyp 5557851f) showed NO_EDGE due to maxBarsHeld=12 forcing exit at 12h. Extended-maxBarsHeld null-screen (hyp 958735fb) is testing 1h with maxBarsHeld=24-96. This hypothesis tests a structurally different approach: at the 4h timeframe, each bar = 4 hours. Default maxBarsHeld=12 on 4h bars = 48 hours of hold time. ETH mean-reversion from EMA200-extreme to EMA50 typically completes in 24-96h. MECHANISM: The 4h timeframe naturally provides the hold duration that the 1h timeframe requires parameter extension to achieve. Because 4h bars also filter out intraday noise (ETH 1h has higher noise-to-signal), the 4h surface may show superior Sharpe at the same mean-reversion holding period. AXES: extremeAtrMult[1.5-3.0] x stopAtrBuffer[0.6-1.8] x rsiOversoldMax[20-35] (3D). This uses the default maxBarsHeld=12 (=48h on 4h bars). Portfolio fit: mean-reversion is mechanistically opposite to VBO (breakout-expansion) — expected near-zero or negative Spearman correlation. Null-screen approach: EDGE_PRESENT criterion P75(PF)>=1.2 AND share(PF>=1.2)>=0.25.

structured_data.motivation: 4h bar size naturally provides 48h of hold time at default maxBarsHeld=12, adequate for ETH mean-reversion completion
